---
title: "Install and Get Started"
description: "Minimal install notes and first-run routes for np, npRmpi, and crs."
keywords:
  - install np
  - install npRmpi
  - install crs
  - quickstart
  - RStudio
  - MPI
---

This page is intentionally short. The goal is to get you from installation to a first successful run without making you read a general introduction to R first.

## Step 1: install R and an editor

You will need a working installation of [R](https://www.r-project.org/) and, if you prefer an IDE, [RStudio / Posit Desktop](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/).

## Step 2: install the package you want

For `np` and `crs`, installation from CRAN is straightforward.

```r
## Install the two serial packages directly from CRAN
install.packages("np")
install.packages("crs")
```

For `npRmpi`, installation depends on the MPI implementation available on your system. The current platform and mode guidance lives on [MPI and Large Data](mpi_large_data.qmd).

## Step 2a: if you need `npRmpi`, choose the right first run

- On macOS and Linux, start with the `session` / `spawn` quickstart unless you already know you need `mpiexec`.
- On Windows, start with `attach` mode under `mpiexec`; the current package source explicitly treats that as the supported entry path rather than `spawn`.
- If you need explicit broadcast control or a heterogeneous-cluster workflow, use the `profile` route on [MPI and Large Data](mpi_large_data.qmd).

## Step 2b: use the smallest runnable script first

The quickest way to see working code is now [Quickstarts](quickstarts.qmd). That page shows the small starter scripts inline so you can copy them directly without downloading anything first.

## Step 3: test that the package loads

These are simple first checks.

```r
## Load the serial kernel package
library(np)
```

```r
## Load the spline package
library(crs)
```

## A small first run in `np`

If you want a very small end-to-end example, this is a good place to begin.

```r
## Fit and plot the smallest local-constant regression example
library(np)
data(cps71, package = "np")

model.lc <- npreg(logwage ~ age, data = cps71)
plot(model.lc)
```

## A small first run in `npRmpi`

```r
## Start a one-worker session, then fit the same first model in npRmpi
library(npRmpi)
npRmpi.init(nslaves = 1)
data(cps71, package = "npRmpi")

model.lc <- npreg(logwage ~ age, data = cps71)
plot(model.lc)

npRmpi.quit()
```

## A small first run in `crs`

```r
## Fit and plot the analogous spline model in crs
library(crs)
data(cps71, package = "crs")

model.crs <- crs(logwage ~ age, data = cps71)
plot(model.crs, mean = TRUE)
```

## What next?

- If you are deciding between packages, go to [Choose a Package](primer.qmd).
- If you want the smallest runnable scripts first, go to [Quickstarts](quickstarts.qmd).
- If you want more kernel examples, go to [Kernel Methods](np_npRmpi.qmd).
- If you need MPI, go to [MPI and Large Data](mpi_large_data.qmd).
- If you want downloadable `npRmpi` quickstarts, go to [Code Catalog](code_catalog.qmd).
- If you want longer scripts, go to [Worked Examples](regression.qmd).
